test.note: 692 passed; 0 failed; 3 ignored. Default cargo test fails on Linux due to V8 TLS relocations with cdylib crate type --lib is required

nonBlockingIssues.description: MessageStart handler emits empty-string MessageDelta for assistant-role messages, adding noise to the event stream. Consider using ItemStarted alone as the message-start signal.
subscribe()/event_receiver() use blocking_lock() fallback which is a latent deadlock risk in async context. Current usage is safe but fragile.
Toolcall delta/end events lose call_id correlation (use empty string). Downstream consumers relying on call_id for correlation would break.
is_connected() uses Mutex try_lock instead of AtomicBool, inconsistent with PiNativeTransport. False positives possible during disconnect.
Session timestamps (created_at, updated_at) are always empty strings because Pi doesn't store timestamps in a standard format.
Detection probe functions (probe_pi_binary, probe_pi_version, probe_pi_acp) have zero direct unit test coverage — only tested via data structure tests.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated provider-transport.md with Pi ACP transport architecture, detection, session persistence, transport preference, test coverage, and known testing gotchas (combined lifecycle test hang, cargo test --lib requirement on Linux)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note about using `cargo test --lib` instead of plain `cargo test` for the codex-mobile-client crate on Linux, since the cdylib crate type causes V8 TLS relocation linker errors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both pi-native-transport and pi-acp-transport workers discovered this independently and worked around it. The services.yaml test command also needs updating.

rejectedObservations.observation: AgentPermissionPolicy was changed from manual Default impl to derive Default with #[default] attribute — convention change
rejectedObservations.reason: Minor style improvement, not systemic, not worth a guidance update
